facts.primaryTitle: What Really Happened at Dyatlov Pass?
facts.setting: Nine experienced hikers cut their tent open from the inside and fled into deadly cold without proper clothing.
criticalEvents: Nine experienced hikers cut their tent open from the inside and fled into deadly cold without proper clothing.
The question is not whether they were frightened.
It is what made the tent feel more dangerous than the storm.
the nine members of Igor Dyatlov's hiking group was experienced Soviet trekkers when the story began in the northern Ural Mountains in February 1959.
facts.threat: Nine experienced hikers cut their tent open from the inside and fled into deadly cold without proper clothing.
facts.primaryReveal: The final warning is therefore simple: when the same impossible detail appears twice, do not wait for a third occurrence to prove that it is real.
facts.finalConsequence: The final warning is therefore simple: when the same impossible detail appears twice, do not wait for a third occurrence to prove that it is real.
facts.unresolvedQuestion: Did the nine members of Igor Dyatlov's hiking group defeat the thing at the centre of the story, or did the encounter simply teach it how to remain hidden?
